title: Getting started
text: netCDF data grids created using function makeNetCDF_file. requires user input following: ncdfFilename define netCDF file created. Note, using default compression settings, meteorological variable requires ~5GB hard-drive storage full record (1900 now). Additionally, netCDF files stored locally, network, minimise time data extraction. updateFrom : start date data. Users often set default date .Date('1900-01-01') extract available data. updateTo : end date data. Users often set default date two days ago extract available data. urlPrecip etc define meteorological variables input netCDF data grids. Setting NA causes variable included netCDF data grids. See example build first netCDF data file.

title: Getting started
text: netCDF data grids created, extract data using extractCatchmentData function. essential user inputs : ncdfFilename  file name pointing netCDF file. locations defining locations data xtracted. can full file name ESRI shape file points polygons shape file already imported using readShapeSpatial(). Either way shape file must long/lat (.e. projected), use ellipsoid GRS 80, first column must unique ID. Many user controls available control duration, time-step, spatial temporal aggregation (e.g. mean, sum, min max), meteorological variables required measure evapotranspiration. examples time-series extractions see . examples spatial data extractions see ).

title: Make source data grids
text: example shows steps required build netCDF data grid file. First, let’s define dates data grids. data grids created data dates updateTo updateTo. latter two dates input data downloaded 1/1/1900 yesterday. Note, practice users often run makeNetCDF_file build netCDF data file contain variables entire record length (requires ~5GB disk storage) use netCDF grids multiple projects, rather re-building netCDF grids project. Next file name netCDF grids need defined. ’ll just use temporary file. change non-temporary file name folder. Now ’re ready download build netCDF grids. netCDF data file contains grids daily rainfall, minimum maximum temperature. , updated include vapour pressure solar radiation Australia. Now let’s get summary netCDF file ’ve created. Note output data.frame shows expected variables expected date range.
code: startDate <- as.Date(Sys.Date()-15,"%Y-%m-%d") endDate <- as.Date(Sys.Date()-5,"%Y-%m-%d") ncdfFilename <- tempfile(fileext='.nc') ncdffile.name <- makeNetCDF_file(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,                 updateFrom=startDate, updateTo=endDate,                 vars = c('precip','tmin','tmax')) #> ... Testing downloading of each variable. #>     Testing precip grid data. #>     Testing tmin grid data. #>     Testing tmax grid data. #> ... NetCDF file will be updated as follows: #>        - New variables to add: precip  tmin  tmax #>        - Existing variables to modify: (none) #>        - Data will be updated from  2026-04-09  to  2026-04-19 #> ... Downloading data for each variable and importing to netcdf file: #> Data construction FINISHED. #> Summary of time points successfully imported (and errors). #>        Imported Errors #> precip       11      0 #> tmin         11      0 #> tmax         11      0 #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:25 summary.df <- AWAPer::file.summary(ncdffile.name) summary.df #>        group   var.string       from         to time.step #> precip grid1 grid1/precip 2026-04-09 2026-04-19      days #> tmin   grid1   grid1/tmin 2026-04-09 2026-04-19      days #> tmax   grid1   grid1/tmax 2026-04-09 2026-04-19      days #>                                    time.datum  units              ellipsoid.crs #> precip days since 1900-01-01 00:00:00.0 -0:00 mm/day +proj=longlat +ellps=GRS80 #> tmin   days since 1900-01-01 00:00:00.0 -0:00  deg_C +proj=longlat +ellps=GRS80 #> tmax   days since 1900-01-01 00:00:00.0 -0:00  deg_C +proj=longlat +ellps=GRS80

what: Extract daily precipitation and PET data
title: Extract daily area weighted PET and precipitation
text: daily area weighted average precipitation PET extracted across two catchments. data extracted netCDF file ncdfFilename dates extract.extract.. AWAPer variables extracted need calculation Morton’s PET. Note netCDF files must working directory full file path must given. Morton’s wet-environment areal evapotranspiration estimated (options available). use Morton’s CREA formulation defined setting ET.function variable ‘ET.MortonCRAE’. calculation areal wet environment PET defined setting Morton’s specific variable ET.Mortons.est ‘wet areal ET’ (note, options exist within AWAPer variables). Lastly, following McMahon et al, (2013), Morton’s PET calculated monthly time step (daily) improve reliability. monthly estimate interpolated daily. estimation ET uses evapotranspiration package. requires set constants, loaded follows. area weighted daily meteorological variables PET can calculated follows, given includes . Note, two months extracted, default method handle missing data anomalies (e.g. Tmin > Tmax) ET calculations automatically changed default . Next time series extracted daily precipitation PET catchment plotted. precipitation plots show area weighted estimate spatial variability day (+/- one standard deviation).
code: data(constants,package='Evapotranspiration') climateData.daily = extractCatchmentData(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,                       extractFrom=date.from,                       extractTo=date.to,                       vars = c('tmax', 'tmin', 'precip', 'vprp', 'solarrad', 'et'),                       locations=catchments,                       temporal.timestep = 'daily',                       temporal.function.name='sum',                       spatial.function.name='var',                       ET.function='ET.MortonCRAE',                       ET.timestep = 'monthly',                       ET.Mortons.est='wet areal ET',                       ET.constants= constants) #> Extraction data summary: #>     NetCDF climate data exists from 2010-07-01 to 2010-10-31 #>     Data will be extracted from  2010-07-01  to  2010-10-31  at  2  locations #>     WARNING: The extraction duration is < 2 years and getET = TRUE. #>              Hence, ET.missing_method and ET.abnormal_method is changed to "neighbouring average". #> Starting data extraction: #> ... Building catchment weights for each grid. #> Loading required namespace: ncdf4 #> ... Extracted DEM elevations from AWS (using tmax coordinate and a GRS80 ellipsoid). #> Mosaicing & Projecting #> Note: Elevation units are in meters #> ... Starting to extract data across all variable and locations: #> ... Linearly interpolating gaps #> ... Backfilling dates prior to the start of observations #> ... Calculate daily ET at each grid cell. #> ... Calculating area weighted results at required time-step. #> Data extraction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:26 par(mfrow=c(2,1), mar =  c(5, 7.5, 4, 2.7) + 0.1)  # Loop through each catchment and plot the daily precipitation and PET. for (i in 1:length(catchments$CatchID)) {    filt = climateData.daily$temporal.sum$Location.ID == catchments$CatchID[i]    # Convert year, month and day columns from extractions to a date.   climateData.daily.date = as.Date(paste0(climateData.daily$temporal.sum$year[filt], "-",                            climateData.daily$temporal.sum$month[filt], "-",                            climateData.daily$temporal.sum$day[filt]))     # Plot precipitation and standard deviation against observations   # ---------------------------------------------------------   max.y = max(climateData.daily$temporal.sum$precip[filt] +         sqrt(climateData.daily$spatial.var$precip[filt]))    # Precipitation   plot(climateData.daily.date,         climateData.daily$temporal.sum$precip[filt],         type = "h", col = "#e31a1c", lwd = 3, mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0),         main=paste('Catchment ID',catchments$CatchID[i]),         ylim = c(0, 80), ylab = "", xlab = "2010", xaxs = "i", yaxt = "n", bty = "l", yaxs = "i")    axis(side = 2, mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0), line = 0.5, at = seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 20),         labels = c("0", "20", "40", "60", "80mm"), col = "#e31a1c", col.axis = "#e31a1c")    # Standard deviation   for (j in 1:length(climateData.daily.date)) {     x.plot = rep(climateData.daily.date[j], 2)     y.plot = c(climateData.daily$temporal.sum$precip[filt][j] +         sqrt(climateData.daily$spatial.var$precip[filt][j]),         climateData.daily$temporal.sum$precip[filt][j] -         sqrt(climateData.daily$spatial.var$precip[filt][j]))     lines(x.plot, y.plot, col = "black", lwd = 1.2)   }    # Plot evap data.   par(new = TRUE)   plot(climateData.daily.date, climateData.daily$temporal.sum$et[filt],       col = "#bc80bd", lwd = 2, ylab = "", ylim = c(0, 4), lty = 1,       xlab = "", xaxs = "i", yaxt = "n", xaxt = "n", type = "l", bty = "n", yaxs = "i")    axis(side = 2, line = 2.3, mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0), labels = c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4mm"),       at = seq(from = 0, to = 4, by = 1), col = "#bc80bd", col.axis = "#bc80bd")    legend("topleft", cex = 0.8, lwd = 2, bty = "n", inset = c(0.01, -0.01),       lty = c(1, 1), pch = c(NA, NA),       col = c("#e31a1c",  "#bc80bd"),       legend = c("Precipitation (bars +/- one standard dev.)", "Morton CRAE PET"), xpd = NA) }

what: Extract and map monthly total precipitation and PET
title: Extract daily area weighted PET and precipitation
text: meteorological spatial data can also extracted required timescale. monthly total precipitation PET across two catchments mapped. extract spatial data, statistic spatial data sum, variable spatial.function.name set ’’. extract monthly totals, temporal.timestep set ‘monthly’ temporal.function.name ‘sum’. former, options daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly annual. latter, built-function user-defined function accepts single vector data returns single number work. First let’s extract precipitation. monthly total precipitation can mapped show spatial variability.  Next let’s extract variables calculate PET map. monthly total PET can mapped show spatial variability. map shows considerable spatial variability. Much variability emerges point PET catchment centroid used.  Now maps rainfall PET, can calculate map monthly rainfall deficit.

Building catchment weights for each grid. #> ... Starting to extract data across all variable and locations: #> ... Linearly interpolating gaps #> ... Backfilling dates prior to the start of observations #> ... Calculating area weighted results at required time-step. #> Data extraction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:03 v = list("sp.polygons", catchments, col = "red",first=FALSE) colInd = which(startsWith(colnames(PrecipData.monthly@data), "precip_")) sp::spplot(PrecipData.monthly,colInd, sp.layout = list(v),         colorkey = list(title = "Precip (mm/month)")) metData.monthly = extractCatchmentData(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,                      extractFrom=date.from,                      extractTo=date.to,                      vars = c('tmax', 'tmin', 'precip', 'vprp', 'solarrad', 'et'),                      locations=catchments,                      spatial.function.name = '',                      temporal.timestep = 'monthly',                      temporal.function.name = 'sum',                      ET.function = 'ET.MortonCRAE',                      ET.timestep = 'monthly',                      ET.Mortons.est='wet areal ET',                      ET.constants= constants) #> Extraction data summary: #>     NetCDF climate data exists from 2010-07-01 to 2010-10-31 #>     Data will be extracted from  2010-07-01  to  2010-10-31  at  2  locations #>     WARNING: The extraction duration is < 2 years and getET = TRUE. #>              Hence, ET.missing_method and ET.abnormal_method is changed to "neighbouring average". #> Starting data extraction: #> ... Extracted DEM elevations from AWS (using tmax coordinate and a GRS80 ellipsoid). #> Mosaicing & Projecting #> Note: Elevation units are in meters #> ... Starting to extract data across all variable and locations: #> ... Linearly interpolating gaps #> ... Backfilling dates prior to the start of observations #> ... Calculate daily ET at each grid cell. #> ... Calculating area weighted results at required time-step. #> Data extraction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:25 colInd = which(startsWith(colnames(metData.monthly@data), "et_")) sp::spplot(metData.monthly,colInd, sp.layout = list(v),   colorkey = list(title = "PET (mm/month)")) colnames.all = colnames(metData.monthly@data) colInd.P = which(startsWith(colnames.all, "precip_")) colInd.PET = which(startsWith(colnames.all, "et_"))  for (i in 1:length(colInd.P)) {   ind.P = colInd.P[i]   ind.PET = colInd.PET[i]   colname.P = colnames.all[ ind.P ]   colname.tmp = sub('precip_','Deficit_mm_',colname.P)   metData.monthly[[colname.tmp]] = metData.monthly[[ind.P]] - metData.monthly[[ind.PET]] }  colInd = which(startsWith(colnames(metData.monthly@data), "Deficit_mm_")) sp::spplot(metData.monthly,colInd, sp.layout = list(v),   colorkey = list(title = "Rainfall deficit, P - PET (mm/month)"))

what: Point versus area weighted rainfall deficit
title: Extract daily area weighted PET and precipitation
text: area weighted catchment rainfall deficit estimates compared point estimates centtoid catchment. First, centroids estimated using simple bounds box method. , coordinates converted spatial object set projection GDA94. Next, meteorological data PET derived using area weighted approach centroids. Now let’s compare two estimates precipitation, PET rainfall deficit. right plots show rainfall deficit. shows using centroid estimate introduces errors 6 mm/month error.

title: Extract water year area weighted precipitation
text: Next let’s extract calendar year area weighted total precipitation across two catchments. data extracted dates extract.extract.. output time step can estimated daily monthly source data, can extracted time. Now let’s also estimate uncertainty calender year rainfall using root mean square interpolation error (RMSE). Specifically, assuming error unbiased, allows us assume RMSE equals standard deviation. Taking square gives us variance, can sum water year. calender year data extracted, total annual precipitation plotted.
code: climateData.annual = extractCatchmentData(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,                       extractFrom=date.from,                       extractTo=date.to,                       vars = c('precip', 'precip.monthly'),                       locations=catchments,                       temporal.timestep = 'annual',                       temporal.function.name='sum',                       spatial.function.name='var') #> Loading required namespace: sp #> Extraction data summary: #>     NetCDF climate data exists from 2010-01-01 to 2015-12-31 #>     Data will be extracted from  2010-01-01  to  2015-12-31  at  2  locations #> Starting data extraction: #> ... Building catchment weights for each grid. #> Loading required namespace: ncdf4 #> ... Starting to extract data across all variable and locations: #> ... Linearly interpolating gaps #> ... Backfilling dates prior to the start of observations #> ... Calculating area weighted results at required time-step. #> Data extraction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:01:01 sqrd.sum <- function(x) {return(sum(x^2))}  climateData.annual.err = extractCatchmentData(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,                       extractFrom=date.from,                       extractTo=date.to,                       vars = c('precip.RMSE'),                       locations=catchments,                       temporal.timestep = 'annual',                       temporal.function.name = sqrd.sum,                       spatial.function.name = 'var') #> Extraction data summary: #>     NetCDF climate data exists from 2010-01-01 to 2015-12-31 #>     Data will be extracted from  2010-01-01  to  2015-12-31  at  2  locations #> Starting data extraction: #> ... Building catchment weights for each grid. #> ... Starting to extract data across all variable and locations: #> ... Linearly interpolating gaps #> ... Backfilling dates prior to the start of observations #> ... Calculating area weighted results at required time-step. #> Data extraction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:51 par(mfrow=c(2,1), mar =  c(5, 7.5, 4, 2.7) + 0.1)  # Loop through each catchment and plot the daily precipitation and PET. for (i in 1:length(catchments$CatchID)) {    filt = climateData.annual$temporal.sum$Location.ID == catchments$CatchID[i]    # Plot precipitation from monthly data   tmp.date = climateData.annual$temporal.sum[filt,]   x.data = tmp.date$year   y.data = tmp.date$precip.monthly    # Plot calendar year precipitation from daily data   plot(x = x.data,        y = y.data,        type = "l",        col = "red",        lwd = 1.2,        mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0),        ylim = c(0, ceiling(max(y.data) * 1.05)),        ylab = "Precip. [mm/year]",        xlab = "Calender year",        xaxs = "r",        bty = "l",        yaxs = "r",        main=paste('Catchment ID',catchments$CatchID[i]))    # Get water year data from daily data   tmp.date = climateData.annual$temporal.sum[filt,]   y.data = tmp.date$precip   tmp.date = climateData.annual.err$temporal.sqrd.sum[filt,]   y.data.low = y.data - 2*sqrt(tmp.date$precip.RMSE)   y.data.hi = y.data + 2*sqrt(tmp.date$precip.RMSE)    lines(x = x.data,        y = y.data,        type = "l",        col = "blue",        lwd = 1.2)    # Add interpolation error bars for water years precipitation from daily data   arrows(x0 = x.data,          y0 = y.data.low,          x1 = x.data,          y1 = y.data.hi,          angle=90,          code=3,          length=0.06,          col="black")    # Add legend   legend("bottomleft",          lwd = 2,          bty = "n",          inset = c(0.01, -0.01),          lty = c(1, 1, 1), pch = c(NA, NA, NA),          col = c("blue",  "black", "red"),          legend = c("From daily data", "5-95 %ile error", "From monthly data"),          xpd = NA,          cex=0.75) }

title: Catchment Area Weighted Climate Data Anywhere in Australia
text: Getting rainfall evaporation time-series data essential first step many hydrological studies. often requires tedious task finding weather station sufficiently close study catchment. Rarely weather station inside catchment, even spatial variability rainfall evaporation throughout catchment must ignores - errors introduced (see example ). AWAPer eliminates challenges allowing efficient estimate area weighted (.e. catchment weighted) weather time-series data anywhere Australia time step. features include: Area weighted point precipitation, minimum maximum temperature, vapour pressure solar radiation anywhere Australia time-step. See examples . Maps catchment weather variables time showing spatial variability precipitation potential evapotranspiration (see examples ) Ten measures area weighted point evapotranspiration (see examples ) using Evaporation package, implementing McMahon et al, (2013), meteorological data sourced Australian Bureau Meteorology (BOM) ~5x5km gridded products (see ). package functions building compressed netCDF grids BOM data. Users generally build netCDF grids using historic data update required (see example ). package development funded Victorian Government Department Environment, Land, Water Planning Climate Water Initiate. details approach see function references journal paper Peterson et al. (2020).

title: Extract data over catchment area and duration . — extractCatchmentData
text: ncdfFilename full file name (string) netCDF file. extractFrom date string specifying start date data extraction. default "1900-1-1". extractTo date string specifying end date data extraction. default today's date YYYY-MM-DD. vars vector variables names extract. available variables : daily precipitation, daily minimum temperature, daily maximum temperature, daily 3pm vapour pressure grids daily solar radiation evapotranspiration. input vector options c('tmax', 'tmin', 'precip', 'precip.monthly', 'vprp', 'solarrad', 'et'). Importantly, input et calculated available gridded data (see ET. inputs ). calculate ET, required inputs calculation ET must also extracted (.e. input generally c('tmax', 'tmin', 'precip', 'vprp', 'solarrad', 'et'). defaults available. default '' result variables netCDF file provided rownames(AWAPer::file.summary(ncdfFilename)). locations either full file name ESRI shape file points polygons (latter assumed catchment boundaries) shape file already imported using readShapeSpatial(). Either way shape file must long/lat (.e. projected), use ellipsoid GRS 80, first column must unique ID. temporal.timestep character string time step output data. options daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual  user-defined index , say, water-years (see xts::period.apply). default daily. temporal.function.name character string function name applied aggregate daily data temporal.timestep. Note, NA values removed aggregation calculation. required consider writing function. default mean. spatial.function.name character string function name applied estimate daily spatial spread variable. NA "" locations polygon, spatial data returned. default var. interp.method character string defining method interpolating gridded data (see raster::extract). options : 'simple', 'bilinear' ''. default ''. set interpolation 'simple' locations polygon(s) 'bilinear' locations points. missing.method three character vector settings fill gaps source data. three inputs control following. # 1) infilling small holes source grids using focal(), takes mean non-NA surrounding grid cells. user input controls maximum hole size filled, units number grid cells. hole greater input, gap within hole remain. default maximum hole size infilled 5x5 grid cells. 2) Gaps remain hole infilling, time steps observations, interpolated time. gaps observations prior gap interpolated. interpolation method user-defined includes 'constant', 'linear', 'fmm', 'periodic', 'natural', 'monoH.FC' 'hyman'. default 'linear'. See approx splinefun details. 3) Gaps remain (often due extraction date prior start observation record variable) estimated , say, mean day year. Specifically, extracted observed data allocated calender day. , say, ten years daily data day year ten observations. gaps corresponding calender day assigned value user-defined function observations (NB: one observed value exists day, observed value returned). default function mean. standard functions (e.g. median) user defined functions can used. default input c('5', 'linear', 'mean'). gap filling method can turned input c('0', '', ''), useful identify interpolated data points. ET.function character string evapotranspiration function used. methods can derived AWAP data ET.Abtew, ET.HargreavesSamani, ET.JensenHaise, ET.Makkink, ET.McGuinnessBordne, ET.MortonCRAE , ET.MortonCRWE, ET.Turc. Default ET.MortonCRAE .e.  complementary relationship areal evapotranspiration . ET.DEM.res zoom resolution land surface elevation required calculate ET. elevatr package used extract elevation (metres) AWS Open Data Terrain Tiles. input controls zoom resolution. Higher values increase accuracy, significantly slower. See details. Default 10. ET.Mortons.est character string type Morton's ET estimate. ET.MortonCRAE, options potential ET,wet areal ET actual areal ET. ET.MortonCRWE, options potential ET shallow lake ET. default wet areal ET, ET.function = 'ET.MortonCRAE' provides estimate wet areal potential evapotranspiration. ET.Turc.humid logical variable Turc function using humid adjustment.See ET.Turc. now fixed F. ET.timestep character string evapotranpiration time step. Options daily,  monthly, annual options dependent upon chosen ET.function. default 'monthly'. ET.missing_method character string interpolation method missing variables required ET calculation. options 'monthly average', 'seasonal average', 'DoY average' 'neighbouring average'. Default 'DoY average' extraction duration less two years, default 'neighbouring average'. See ReadInputs ET.abnormal_method character string interpolation method abnormal variables required ET calculation (e.g. Tmin > Tmax). Options defaults ET.missing_method. See ReadInputs ET.constants list constants Evapotranspiration package required ET calculations. get data use command data(constants). Default list().

title: Extract data over catchment area and duration . — extractCatchmentData
code: # The example shows how to extract and save data. #--------------------------------------- library(sp)  # Set dates for building netCDFs and extracting data. # Note, to reduce runtime this is done only a fortnight (14 days). startDate = as.Date("2000-01-01","%Y-%m-%d") endDate = as.Date("2000-01-14","%Y-%m-%d")  # Set names for netCDF file. ncdfFilename = tempfile(fileext='.nc')  # Build netCDF grids and over a defined time period. # Only precip data is to be added to the netCDF files. # This is because the URLs for the other variables are set to zero. # \donttest{ file.name = makeNetCDF_file(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,              updateFrom=startDate,              updateTo=endDate,              vars = c('precip')) #> ... Testing downloading of each variable. #>     Testing precip grid data. #> ... NetCDF file will be updated as follows: #>        - New variables to add: precip   #>        - Existing variables to modify: (none) #>        - Data will be updated from  2000-01-01  to  2000-01-14 #> ... Downloading data for each variable and importing to netcdf file: #> Data construction FINISHED. #> Summary of time points successfully imported (and errors). #>        Imported Errors #> precip       14      0 #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:11  # Load example catchment boundaries and remove all but the first. # Note, this is done only to speed up the example runtime. data("catchments") catchments = catchments[1,]  # Extract daily precip. data (not Tmin, Tmax, VPD, ET). # Note, the input "locations" can also be a file to a ESRI shape file. climateData = extractCatchmentData(ncdfFilename=file.name,               extractFrom=startDate,               extractTo=endDate,               vars = c('precip'),               locations=catchments,               temporal.timestep = 'daily') #> Extraction data summary: #>     NetCDF climate data exists from 2000-01-01 to 2000-01-14 #>     Data will be extracted from  2000-01-01  to  2000-01-14  at  1  locations  #> Starting data extraction: #> ... Building catchment weights for each grid. #> Loading required namespace: ncdf4 #> ... Starting to extract data across all variable and locations: #> ... Linearly interpolating gaps #> ... Backfilling dates prior to the start of observations #> ... Calculating area weighted results at required time-step. #> Data extraction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:04  # Extract the daily catchment average data. climateDataAvg = climateData$catchmentTemporal.mean  # Extract the daily catchment variance data. climateDataVar = climateData$catchmentSpatial.var  # Remove temp. files unlink(ncdfFilename) # }

text: makeNetCDF_file creates one netCDF file daily climate data. One netCDF file created contains precipitation, minimum daily temperature, maximum daily temperature vapour pressure solar radiation data. span 1/1/1900 yesterday requires ~20GB hard-drive space (using default compression). solar radiation, spatial gaps infilled using 3x3 moving average repeated 3 times. minimise runtime extracting data, netCDF file stored locally network drive. Also, building file requires installation 7zip. climate data sourced  Bureau Meteorology Australian Water Availability Project (http://www.bom.gov.au/jsp/awap/.  details see Jones et al. (2009). output function required data extraction functions within package must ran prior. function can used build netCDF file scratch update existing netCDF file previously derived function. build update variable, set respective URL NA.

title: Build a netCDF file of climate data. — makeNetCDF_file
code: # The example shows how to build the netCDF data cubes. # For an additional example see \url{https://github.com/peterson-tim-j/AWAPer/blob/master/README.md} #---------------------------------------  # Set dates for building netCDFs and extracting data from 15 to 5 days ago. startDate = as.Date(Sys.Date()-15,"%Y-%m-%d") endDate = as.Date(Sys.Date()-5,"%Y-%m-%d")  # Set names for the netCDF file (in the system temp. directory). ncdfFilename = tempfile(fileext='.nc')  # \donttest{ # Build netCDF grids for daily precipitation and only over the defined time period. file.names = makeNetCDF_file(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,              updateFrom=startDate,              updateTo=endDate,              vars = c('precip')) #> ... Testing downloading of each variable. #>     Testing precip grid data. #> ... NetCDF file will be updated as follows: #>        - New variables to add: precip   #>        - Existing variables to modify: (none) #>        - Data will be updated from  2026-04-09  to  2026-04-19 #> ... Downloading data for each variable and importing to netcdf file: #> Data construction FINISHED. #> Summary of time points successfully imported (and errors). #>        Imported Errors #> precip       11      0 #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:08  # Now, to demonstrate updating the netCDF grids to one day ago, rerun with # the same file names but \code{updateFrom=NA}. file.names = makeNetCDF_file(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,              updateFrom=NA) #> ... Testing downloading of each variable. #>     Testing precip grid data. #> ... updateFrom reduced to ensure all variables have the same start date. #> ... updateTo increased to ensure all variables have the same end date. #> ... NetCDF file will be updated as follows: #>        - New variables to add: (none) #>        - Existing variables to modify: precip   #>        - Data will be updated from  2026-04-09  to  2026-04-19 #> ... Downloading data for each variable and importing to netcdf file: #> Data construction FINISHED. #> Summary of time points successfully imported (and errors). #>        Imported Errors #> precip       11      0 #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:06   # Remove temp. file  unlink(ncdfFilename) # }

title: AWAPer 0.1.60
text: Major improvements robustness data sources handled. data now stored one netCDF files. achieved using netCDF groups. source data attributes (e.g. url, time step, spatial datum) now longer hard coded, rather supplied get.variableSource(). allows updating source data information without package changes. Monthly source precipitation grids now handled (see [] (https://www.bom.gov.au/climate/austmaps/-agcd-maps.shtml) details monthly gridded data). Vignettes extended better evaluate results rain gauge data demonstrate new features. Stronger error handling input dates ET settings. Summary build netcDF file now provided file.summary()

title: AWAPer 0.1.50
text: Functional removed CRAN BOM download issues! 🎉 extractCatchmentData function modified longer require download digital elevation model (DEM) calculating ET. Instead elevatr package used download elevations Amazon Web Service.
